content.intro: Special clauses allow you to add custom terms to tenancy agreements on a per-property basis. They are included automatically whenever an agreement is generated for that property.

steps.title: Open the Property
steps.content: Navigate to the Properties tab and open the property you want to add special clauses to.

steps.title: Find the Special Clauses Section
steps.content: Within the property settings or edit view, locate the Special Clauses section.

steps.title: Enter Your Clause Text
steps.content: Type or paste your custom clause text. Each clause should be clearly worded and specific to the obligations or permissions you want to include.

steps.title: Save
steps.content: Click Save to store the special clauses against the property.

steps.title: Clauses Included in Agreements Automatically
steps.content: When you generate any tenancy agreement for this property, the special clauses will be included automatically in the agreement document — you do not need to add them manually each time.

sections.title: No Break Clauses for APTAs
sections.content: Under the Renters Rights Act 2026, break clauses are not permitted in Assured Periodic Tenancy Agreements (post May 2026). Do not add break clause language for any new tenancies starting on or after 1 May 2026.

sections.title: Common Uses for Special Clauses
sections.content: Special clauses are suitable for a wide range of additional terms, including: no smoking within the property, requirement for professional end-of-tenancy cleaning, garden maintenance obligations, pet agreements (specifying permitted pets and any conditions), and any specific landlord requirements for the property.
